title: Siem Reap 14-Day Cultural Tour
metaTitle: Siem Reap 14-Day Cultural Tour | Flights, Hotels & Tours Included
metaDescription: Book a 14-day trip to Siem Reap, Cambodia: round-trip flights, hotel, breakfast, transfers, and guided tours including Angkor Wat and Angkor Archaeological Park 3-day pass. Prices from $1866 per person (estimate).
highlights: Sunrise cycling tour of Angkor temples
overview: Spend 14 days discovering Siem Reap, Cambodia — Tropical climate with a hot dry season (Nov-Apr, peaking in April) and a humid wet season (May-Oct) with heavy but often brief afternoon rains. November to February for cooler, dry weather ideal for temple exploring. This itinerary blends Angkor Wat, Angkor Archaeological Park 3-day pass, Old Market (Psar Chas) with free time to explore at your own pace, backed by round-trip flights, handpicked accommodation, and a dedicated travel consultant from enquiry through departure.
included: Round-trip economy-class airfare from your selected origin city (upgrade to premium economy or business available)
13 nights accommodation in your selected category (Budget, Mid-range, Premium, or Luxury)
Daily local transport as per the itinerary
Guided tours including Angkor Wat, Angkor Archaeological Park 3-day pass, Old Market (Psar Chas)
All entrance fees for attractions and activities listed in the itinerary
Applicable taxes and OTA service fee
Dedicated travel consultant support from booking through departure
notIncluded: Visa fees and travel document costs (see Visa Information)
Travel insurance (strongly recommended, available as an add-on)
Lunches and dinners except where noted in the itinerary
Optional activities not listed in the day-by-day itinerary
Airline seat selection and baggage upgrades
Any fare difference for premium economy or business class upgrades

itinerary.title: Arrive in Siem Reap
itinerary.description: Land at Siem Reap Angkor International Airport (SAI) and take your included private transfer to your hotel. After settling in, ease into the trip with a relaxed visit to Angkor Wat — Iconic 12th-century temple, part of the Angkor Pass. Round off the evening with a welcome dinner introducing you to local flavors.

itinerary.title: Siem Reap: Angkor Archaeological Park 3-day pass & Bayon Temple
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Angkor Archaeological Park 3-day pass — Covers Angkor Wat, Bayon, Ta Prohm and dozens of temples. In the afternoon, continue to Bayon Temple — Temple famous for giant carved stone faces. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Siem Reap: Ta Prohm & Banteay Srei
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Ta Prohm — "Tomb Raider" temple entwined in jungle roots. In the afternoon, continue to Banteay Srei — Intricately carved pink sandstone temple. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Tonle Sap floating villages
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Tonle Sap floating villages, a popular side trip from Siem Reap. Return to Siem Reap in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Tonle Sap floating villages

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Beng Mealea jungle-swallowed temple — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Siem Reap: Angkor National Museum & Tonle Sap floating village boat tour
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Angkor National Museum — Artifacts and multimedia history of the Khmer Empire. In the afternoon, continue to Tonle Sap floating village boat tour — Boat trip to Kompong Phluk or Chong Khneas. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.
activities: Tonle Sap floating village boat tour

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Beng Mealea temple
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Beng Mealea temple, a popular side trip from Siem Reap. Return to Siem Reap in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Beng Mealea temple

itinerary.title: Siem Reap: Phare, The Cambodian Circus & Cambodia Landmine Museum
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Phare, The Cambodian Circus — Contemporary circus by trained local artists. In the afternoon, continue to Cambodia Landmine Museum — Museum on the country's landmine legacy. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Kbal Spean ("River of a Thousand Lingas") — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.
activities: Kbal Spean ("River of a Thousand Lingas")

itinerary.title: Day Trip to Phnom Kulen National Park
itinerary.description: Full-day excursion to Phnom Kulen National Park, a popular side trip from Siem Reap. Return to Siem Reap in the evening with time free to explore on your own.
activities: Guided day trip: Phnom Kulen National Park

itinerary.title: Siem Reap: Angkor Balloon sunrise ride & Old Market (Psar Chas)
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Angkor Balloon sunrise ride — Tethered hot air balloon over the temples. In the afternoon, continue to Old Market (Psar Chas) — Central market for food, souvenirs, and crafts. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Siem Reap: Pub Street & Wat Bo Pagoda
itinerary.description: Morning visit to Pub Street — Main tourist strip of bars and restaurants. In the afternoon, continue to Wat Bo Pagoda — One of the oldest pagodas in Siem Reap. Evening free to explore local restaurants and nightlife.

itinerary.title: Leisure Day & Optional Activities
itinerary.description: A lighter day to recharge, shop, or add an optional experience. Popular choices include Banteay Srei intricate carvings — your consultant can book add-ons like this on request.

itinerary.title: Departure Day
itinerary.description: Enjoy a final morning at leisure — perhaps one last visit to Angkor Night Market or some souvenir shopping — before your private transfer to SAI for your departure flight.

travelTips: Buy the Angkor Pass (1, 3, or 7-day) at the official ticket center before 5pm the day before for a sunrise visit
Hire a licensed local guide to bring the temple history to life
Start at Angkor Wat before sunrise to beat both heat and crowds
Carry small USD bills, as most transactions are in dollars with riel as change
Pace yourself — this itinerary includes free time so you are not rushing between every attraction.
packingList: Copies of booking confirmations and travel insurance
Lightweight, breathable clothing and a reusable water bottle
Lightweight, breathable, modest clothing that covers shoulders and knees for temples
Sturdy walking shoes for uneven ancient stone steps
Sun protection — hat, sunscreen, and a refillable water bottle
A dust mask or scarf for dry-season road dust
thingsToDo: Tonle Sap floating village boat tour
Sunrise cycling tour of Angkor temples
thingsToAvoid: Unlicensed temple "guides" who overcharge outside the official ticket system
Climbing on fragile ancient carvings or restricted temple structures
Underestimating heat exhaustion — pace your temple-hopping and hydrate
Orphanage or floating-village "tours" run as exploitative tourist traps — book through reputable, ethical operators
topRestaurants: Cuisine Wat Damnak — Modern Khmer fine dining ($$$)
Blue Pumpkin — Bakery and Western-Khmer fusion ($)
Haven Restaurant — Khmer, social-enterprise training restaurant ($$)
instagramSpots: Angkor Wat at sunrise across the reflecting pool
hiddenGems: Kbal Spean ("River of a Thousand Lingas")
familyActivities: Tonle Sap floating village boat tour
adventureActivities: Sunrise cycling tour of Angkor temples
Hot air balloon ride over Angkor
luxuryExperiences: Private sunrise tour of Angkor Wat with a personal guide
Suite stay at a colonial-era resort like Raffles Grand Hotel d'Angkor
Private helicopter flight over the Angkor complex
Fine dining tasting menu at Cuisine Wat Damnak
honeymoonExperiences: Private candlelit dinner at a boutique resort
Couples spa with traditional Khmer massage
Sunset cruise on Tonle Sap Lake
budgetExperiences: Tuk-tuk day pass around the Small Circuit temples
Street food at the Old Market
Free wandering of Pub Street and the Night Market
Sunrise viewing at Angkor Wat with a shared tuk-tuk
localEtiquette: Cover shoulders and knees when entering Angkor temple complexes.
Never climb on or touch ancient carvings or Buddha statues.
Remove hats and shoes before entering temple sanctuaries.
Do not point your feet toward Buddha images or monks.
Ask permission before photographing monks or local people closely.
localTransportGuide: Tuk-tuks are the primary way to reach the Angkor temples and get around town; negotiate or arrange a full-day driver in advance through your hotel, typically $15-25/day. The Grab app also operates in Siem Reap for cars and tuk-tuks with fixed pricing.
bestTimeToVisit: November to February for cooler, dry weather ideal for temple exploring.
weather: Tropical climate with a hot dry season (Nov-Apr, peaking in April) and a humid wet season (May-Oct) with heavy but often brief afternoon rains.
visaInformation: Most nationalities can obtain a Cambodian e-Visa online in advance or a visa on arrival at the airport for stays up to 30 days; a passport photo and USD cash are typically needed for visa-on-arrival. US dollars are widely accepted alongside the Cambodian riel.
averageFlightTime: ~10.3h from major hubs (estimate; varies by origin — see Suggested Flights)
cancellationPolicy: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.
termsAndConditions: Pricing shown is per person based on the traveler type and tier selected and is an estimate pending live fare and rate confirmation.
Passports must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the return date for most destinations — confirm the exact requirement for your nationality.
Itinerary order may change due to weather, local conditions, or attraction closures; inclusions remain equivalent.
Solo travelers incur a single-room supplement, reflected in the Solo pricing tier.
This package does not include international travel insurance unless explicitly listed under Included.
Prices shown in a currency other than USD are approximate conversions at the rate noted and are not a payment quote.

faqs.question: Do I need a visa to visit Siem Reap?
faqs.answer: Most nationalities can obtain a Cambodian e-Visa online in advance or a visa on arrival at the airport for stays up to 30 days; a passport photo and USD cash are typically needed for visa-on-arrival. US dollars are widely accepted alongside the Cambodian riel.

faqs.question: What is the best time to visit Siem Reap?
faqs.answer: November to February for cooler, dry weather ideal for temple exploring. Tropical climate with a hot dry season (Nov-Apr, peaking in April) and a humid wet season (May-Oct) with heavy but often brief afternoon rains.

faqs.question: What's included in this package?
faqs.answer: Round-trip airfare, accommodation, daily breakfast, airport transfers, daily local transport, and guided tours to the attractions listed in the itinerary. See the Included / Not Included sections for full detail.

faqs.question: Can the itinerary be customized?
faqs.answer: Yes — this itinerary is a starting point. Your travel consultant can add, remove, or reorder activities, extend your stay, or upgrade your hotel category before you book.

faqs.question: What is the cancellation policy?
faqs.answer: Cancellations made 30+ days before departure: full refund minus a $99 processing fee. 15-29 days before departure: 50% refund. 0-14 days before departure or no-show: non-refundable. Airline and hotel supplier cancellation terms may be stricter than this policy and apply in addition — your travel consultant confirms exact terms before payment is taken. No online payment is collected on this site; a consultant confirms final pricing and availability with you directly before any booking is confirmed.

faqs.question: Is Siem Reap safe for tourists?
faqs.answer: Siem Reap is generally safe for tourists who take standard precautions — avoid poorly lit areas at night, keep valuables secure, and use licensed transport.

faqs.question: What currency should I bring to Siem Reap?
faqs.answer: The local currency is KHR. Carry some cash for smaller vendors — card acceptance can be inconsistent outside major hotels and malls.

faqs.question: Is this package good for a honeymoon or family trip?
faqs.answer: This itinerary suits couples, solo travelers, and small groups equally well — talk to your consultant about tailoring it further.

reviews.author: James Williams
reviews.title: Solid mid-range package
reviews.body: Our trip to Siem Reap was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Siem Reap River walk was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Chen Nguyen
reviews.title: Best vacation we've had
reviews.body: Booked this as a couple trip and it was well paced. Pub Street in particular stood out. A couple of the transfers ran a little late but nothing that spoiled the trip.

reviews.author: Fatima Nguyen
reviews.title: Worth every penny
reviews.body: Angkor Archaeological Park 3-day pass alone was worth the trip, but combined with Cambodia Landmine Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Hiroshi Khan
reviews.title: Great for families with kids
reviews.body: Phare, The Cambodian Circus alone was worth the trip, but combined with Angkor Archaeological Park 3-day pass this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Yuki Kowalski
reviews.title: Amazing itinerary, zero stress
reviews.body: Made in Cambodia Market alone was worth the trip, but combined with Cambodia Landmine Museum this itinerary really delivered. Breakfast at the hotel was good every day and the local guide was knowledgeable.

reviews.author: Olivia Novak
reviews.title: Exceeded our expectations
reviews.body: Our trip to Siem Reap was fantastic from start to finish. The hotel was clean and well located, and the guided visit to Wat Preah Prom Rath was a real highlight. Airport transfers were on time both ways.

reviews.author: Sarah Cohen
reviews.title: A little rushed but still wonderful
reviews.body: Siem Reap is stunning and this package covered the essentials well, especially Bayon Temple. The hotel could have been a little closer to the center but transport was easy.

reviews.author: Isabella Santos
reviews.title: Smooth from start to finish
reviews.body: Everything was handled for us — flights, hotel, transfers, and tours to Old Market (Psar Chas) and Bayon Temple. Great communication from the consultant before departure too.

reviews.author: Grace Ibrahim
reviews.title: Adventure of a lifetime
reviews.body: We added a couple of optional activities beyond Angkor Balloon sunrise ride and don't regret it. Good value for what was included, and the food recommendations from our guide were spot on.
